title: The One After The Super Bowl (1)

jokes.text: That commercial always makes me so sad. But then the guy opens his beer and girls run at him... so everything works out okay.
jokes.setup: Joey says the monkey beer commercial makes him sad, implying emotional depth
jokes.punchline: Reveals his sadness is quickly resolved by the shallow gratification of seeing attractive women
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - appears to have depth but immediately undercuts it with superficiality. Good misdirection structure.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That beer commercial with the monkey makes me sad.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It makes me think about how we're all just trying to find our place in the world, you know? Like, what's it all mean?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But then they show those girls in bikinis and I feel better.

jokes.text: And the fact that they're both monkeys?
jokes.setup: Monica lists similarities between Marcel and the commercial monkey (eyes, chin)
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out the obvious commonality everyone missed
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - maximum comedic impact with minimal words. Perfect economy and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: They're both monkeys!

jokes.text: My Malibu Barbie will no longer be wearing white to her wedding.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Marcel's sexual behavior being problematic
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals an unexpectedly specific and disturbing consequence
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and unexpected detail that makes the monkey's behavior both funnier and more horrifying.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's been doing it since he was a baby monkey. I think it's just a natural thing for him.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, he humped my leg!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, he probably thought you were a lady monkey.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not a lady monkey!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, you should stop wearing my perfume then.

jokes.text: Remember when he'd borrow your hat... and when you got it back, there'd be monkey raisins in it?
jokes.setup: Nostalgic tone about Marcel's hat-borrowing
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to monkey defecation
jokes.explanation: 'Monkey raisins' is a clever euphemism that's both cute and disgusting simultaneously.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Remember when Marcel used to borrow my hats?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, and then he'd return them... let's just say not in the same condition.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I know! He'd just... monkey business all over them!

jokes.text: When he did it, it was funny. When I did it to my boss's hat... Suddenly, I had this big 'attitude problem.'
jokes.setup: Building on the monkey raisin story
jokes.punchline: Reveals Chandler tried the same behavior with predictably awful results
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke that reveals Chandler's poor judgment while building on the previous gag.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I tried that once. I thought if I just ignored my problems, they'd go away. So I didn't call my mother for three months.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What happened?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She showed up at my apartment with a suitcase. Said she was moving in until I 'learned to communicate like a human being.'

jokes.text: Rhythm?
jokes.setup: Joey excitedly asks them to guess what he got
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests something Joey clearly lacks
jokes.explanation: Single word perfect comeback that deflates Joey's excitement while referencing his known lack of rhythm.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Guess what I got!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A job?

jokes.text: In Crazy World, that means you're married.
jokes.setup: Joey reads that his stalker enclosed 14 eyelashes in fan mail
jokes.punchline: Chandler frames this as a cultural practice in an imaginary place
jokes.explanation: 'Crazy World' is a perfect invented concept that makes stalker behavior seem like foreign customs.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, in my country, we would consider this a very romantic gesture. Fourteen eyelashes is the traditional dowry.

jokes.text: You are so lucky, I'm gonna share mine.
jokes.setup: Joey is excited about having a stalker
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treats stalkers like a desirable commodity to be shared
jokes.explanation: Very Phoebe response - treating dangerous situations as positive with generous sharing instinct.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I have a stalker!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you have to share!

jokes.text: He will be surprised. Till he realizes he's a monkey... and isn't capable of that emotion.
jokes.setup: Ross plans to surprise Marcel at the zoo
jokes.punchline: Chandler starts supportive then delivers harsh biological reality
jokes.explanation: Good structure that builds hope then crushes it with scientific accuracy delivered deadpan.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's so sweet, Ross. Marcel's gonna love that.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Although, you know, he's a monkey. So he'll probably just throw his poop at you.

jokes.text: Okay, so everyone... pretend I'm telling you a story and it's really funny. So everyone just laugh. Now!
jokes.setup: Phoebe sees the cute guy approaching
jokes.punchline: She orchestrates fake laughter to appear popular/funny
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about performing for an audience, fits Phoebe's quirky social strategies.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God! Ha ha ha ha ha!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That is so funny! Ha ha! You guys are hilarious!

jokes.text: I would love to have kids... hear me play the songs that I will write for them.
jokes.setup: Rob asks if she's considered playing for children
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's awkward pause makes it sound like she wants kids with him first
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's timing creates sexual tension where none was intended, perfectly awkward character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Rob
dialogue.line: Have you considered playing for children?

dialogue.speaker: Rob
dialogue.line: I meant performing music for children's audiences.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! Right, yeah, I'd love to do that.

jokes.text: Hitting her with a frying pan is a good idea. But we want a backup plan, just in case she isn't a cartoon!
jokes.setup: Panic about the stalker approaching their door
jokes.punchline: References cartoon logic while acknowledging real-world consequences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic joke that acknowledges cartoon violence rules don't apply to reality.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God! He's coming! He's actually coming!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What do we do? What do we do?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We could, um, we could draw a door on the wall!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That only works in cartoons, Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I'm willing to try anything at this point!

jokes.text: That's how radio stars escape stalkers!
jokes.setup: Joey's plan to walk past the stalker unrecognized
jokes.punchline: References his minor radio fame as if it's a huge celebrity problem
jokes.explanation: Joey's inflated sense of his own celebrity status, treating minor radio work like major stardom.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'll just walk past her real casual-like, she'll never recognize me.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, she's standing right there.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, come on! I did a commercial! You think I can just walk around the city like a normal person? I had a bit part on a radio show once - I can't even go to the grocery store anymore!

jokes.text: This is it. This is how we're gonna die.
jokes.setup: Footsteps approaching in the hallway
jokes.punchline: Chandler treats fan mail stalker as lethal threat
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation humor, Chandler's dramatic overreaction to minor threat.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, someone's coming! Hide! Hide! They're gonna kill me!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, what? Fan mail? You're upset about fan mail? I thought you were a serial killer!

jokes.text: Sometimes monkeys die. It's not a great saying... but it certainly is fitting today.
jokes.setup: Zoo administrator trying to comfort Ross about Marcel's 'death'
jokes.punchline: Admits his consolation is terrible while doubling down on it
jokes.explanation: Self-aware bad consolation that acknowledges its own inadequacy while persisting with it.

dialogue.speaker: Zoo Administrator
dialogue.line: I know this is hard, but Marcel is in a better place now.

dialogue.speaker: Zoo Administrator
dialogue.line: Actually, that sounded better in my head. But you know what? He's definitely not coming back.

jokes.text: It's a lot cuter if your monkey hasn't just died.
jokes.setup: Offers Ross free admission to bird show with macaws in hats
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges the timing is insensitive given the context
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe humor about inappropriate timing, administrator's social awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Lipson
dialogue.line: I know the timing is insensitive.

jokes.text: You remember Kathy Bates in Misery? Well, she looks the exact opposite of that.
jokes.setup: Describing Joey's stalker's appearance using horror movie reference
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by saying she's the complete opposite
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure that sets up horror expectation then flips it completely.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She looks like something out of a horror movie.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I mean she's beautiful, intelligent, and has a great personality.

jokes.text: Why are you looking at me? He's the one who wants to boff the maniac!
jokes.setup: Monica makes case for stalker's humanity and right to love
jokes.punchline: Gets defensive when others look at her for being too understanding
jokes.explanation: Monica's defensive reaction to being judged for her compassionate but misguided stance.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's still a person! He has feelings! He deserves love just like anyone else!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, he's been following you for three weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So? That doesn't make him a bad person!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Actually, it kind of does.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, don't look at me like that! What? Why is everyone looking at me like that?

jokes.text: That's not a good idea. That's the reason the last guy got fired.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggests imagining audience in underwear to calm nerves
jokes.punchline: Reveals this advice has dangerous precedent at children's library
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that turns innocent performance advice into inappropriate workplace behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Imagine the audience in their underwear.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It really helps with the nervousness.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Although, I have to say, that didn't work out so well at the children's library.

jokes.text: He thinks he can. Oh, what the hell. He can.
jokes.setup: Watching Phoebe about to kiss Rob
jokes.punchline: Chandler's internal debate about Phoebe's chances, settling on optimism
jokes.explanation: Chandler's thought process made audible, showing his supportive but realistic assessment.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Come on, Phoebe, you can do this. You're a beautiful woman. He's gonna kiss you. Any second now. Any second. Come on, Pheebs. Oh my God, just kiss him! Oh my God, just kiss him! Oh my God, just kiss him! Oh my God, just— Oh, he's kissing her! Oh my God, he's kissing her! Oh my God!

jokes.text: Now your mom and your dad said She moved to Peru But the truth is she died And someday you will too
jokes.setup: Phoebe singing cute children's song about grandparents
jokes.punchline: Takes sharp turn into mortality and existential dread
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of children's song format with existential horror. Peak Phoebe character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Grandpa, check out my new job! Grandma, take a nap! You've both got one foot in the grave, so enjoy it while you can! My sweet grandparents, so wise and so cold, buried six feet under when you get too old!

jokes.text: Oh, these hands, these beautiful hands. I could just eat them. But I won't. Otherwise my watch would fall off.
jokes.setup: Stalker rhapsodizing about Joey's surgeon hands
jokes.punchline: Prevents cannibalistic impulse with practical fashion concern
jokes.explanation: Escalates from romantic to cannibalistic to mundanely practical - perfect stalker logic.

dialogue.speaker: Erika
dialogue.line: Your hands are so beautiful. I want to eat them.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, you can't. I just got my nails done.

jokes.text: Because I'm a neurosurgeon... and that was clearly a case of... 'foodal chokage.'
jokes.setup: Joey as fake doctor explaining why he didn't help choking man
jokes.punchline: Invents medical-sounding term for basic choking
jokes.explanation: Joey's attempt to sound medical while clearly not understanding medicine. 'Foodal chokage' is perfectly Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I wanted to help, but the guy had Cardizac Arrest.

jokes.text: There'll be times when you get older When you'll want to sleep with people Just to make them like you But don't!
jokes.setup: Phoebe continuing her children's performance
jokes.punchline: Delivers sex education with anti-prostitution message to kids
jokes.explanation: Another brilliant inappropriate children's song that's actually good advice delivered to wrong audience.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Now, boys and girls, when you grow up, you're going to have questions about sex. That's normal. But remember, sex is a beautiful thing between two people who love each other. And if someone tries to pay you for sex, you say no! Because you are not a prostitute.

jokes.text: It was either 'but' or 'butter.'
jokes.setup: Rob asks how she knew there was a 'but' coming
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's literal interpretation of sensing things
jokes.explanation: Cute Phoebe wordplay showing her literal thinking process and ESP claims.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I have this thing where I can sense when someone's about to say 'but.' It's like a tingling in my spine. First it was just 'but,' then it expanded to 'however,' and now I can sense any word over four letters that implies contradiction.

jokes.text: That's the only thing the zoo's ever told me.
jokes.setup: Conspiracy theorist asks if Ross believes everything the zoo tells him
jokes.punchline: Ross points out he has very limited zoo interaction
jokes.explanation: Ross's logical response deflates conspiracy theory with simple reality check.

dialogue.speaker: Conspiracy Theorist
dialogue.line: You believe everything the zoo tells you?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I have very limited zoo interaction.

jokes.text: Then the farmer hits him And grinds him up And that's how we get hamburgers
jokes.setup: Phoebe starting over with appropriate barnyard animal songs
jokes.punchline: Immediately turns cow song into graphic meat production lesson
jokes.explanation: Phoebe can't help herself - even trying to be appropriate, she delivers agricultural horror to children.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, you know, I'm trying to start over with more appropriate barnyard animal songs.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: The cow says moo. The cow says moo. Moo means the cow is happy and content on the farm.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And then one day, a man comes and puts a gun to its head. Pow! Now the cow is in the freezer section at Whole Foods.

jokes.text: Rachel, it's a world where Joey is a neurosurgeon.
jokes.setup: Rachel confused about soap opera logic
jokes.punchline: Monica points out the fundamental absurdity of the show's premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect summary of soap opera absurdity using Joey's obvious lack of intelligence as the example.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, so the evil twin is actually his good twin's brother, but they were separated at birth by his mother who was actually his aunt?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And now she's married to the man who killed her husband, except he's actually his twin brother so he didn't kill anyone?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Right.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: But that doesn't even make sense!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, you're watching a soap opera. Nothing makes sense. That's the whole point.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Do the people at the hospital know?
jokes.setup: Joey reveals he's an actor, not a real doctor
jokes.punchline: Erika's concern is for the hospital's awareness, not the deception
jokes.explanation: Brilliant stalker logic - worried about hospital administration rather than understanding TV fiction.

dialogue.speaker: Erika
dialogue.line: Wait, you're not a real doctor? You're an actor?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, I was just playing a doctor on TV.

dialogue.speaker: Erika
dialogue.line: Oh my God, the hospital is going to be so upset when they find out!

jokes.text: Nice girl, not great with concept.
jokes.setup: Erika struggling to understand TV vs reality
jokes.punchline: Chandler's diplomatic assessment of her cognitive limitations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler diplomatic insult that's both kind and cutting simultaneously.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm not going to say you're stupid, because that would be mean. But let's just say if they ever need a stunt double for a crash test dummy, you've got the job.

jokes.text: He's not Drake. He's... Hans Ramoray, Drake's evil twin!
jokes.setup: Joey trying to explain he's not Drake
jokes.punchline: Rachel creates even more elaborate soap opera deception
jokes.explanation: Rachel solves reality problem by diving deeper into soap opera tropes - evil twin is perfect escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm not Drake! I'm Joey Tribbiani!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Joey, I know who you are. I was just testing you because I thought you might be Drake in disguise, working undercover to infiltrate our apartment building to steal my cashmere sweaters for a secret fashion line.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? Rachel, that doesn't make any sense.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Exactly what Drake WOULD say. Also, I'm pretty sure my mother isn't actually my mother. She's Drake's stunt double.

jokes.text: And you left the toilet seat up, you bastard!
jokes.setup: Rachel playing Hans's scorned lover
jokes.punchline: Adds mundane domestic complaint to dramatic soap opera scene
jokes.explanation: Perfect blend of soap opera drama with everyday relationship complaints - toilet seat is brilliantly mundane.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Hans, how could you betray me like this? After everything we've shared, all the passion, all the—Hans, you forgot to take out the garbage again!

jokes.text: Who's Barney?
jokes.setup: Rob suggests she doesn't have to be like a purple dinosaur
jokes.punchline: Phoebe doesn't know this major children's character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - being hired for children's music but unfamiliar with biggest children's character.

dialogue.speaker: Rob
dialogue.line: You don't have to be like a purple dinosaur or anything.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What's a purple dinosaur?

jokes.text: What, you never look down in the shower?
jokes.setup: Ross says he hasn't seen his monkey in a year
jokes.punchline: Joey makes penis reference comparing monkey to male anatomy
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey lowbrow humor - perfectly in character but relying on obvious penis joke.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A year? That thing must be huge!

jokes.text: I'm not allowed to make one joke in the 'monkey is penis' genre?
jokes.setup: Others react negatively to his monkey/penis joke
jokes.punchline: Joey defends his right to this specific type of humor
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about joke categories and Joey's artistic freedom to make crude comparisons.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! What? It's funny! A monkey and a guy's... it's a classic setup!

jokes.text: And I have a time share in the Poconos with Flipper.
jokes.setup: Ross claims friendship with monkey to gain access
jokes.punchline: Guard sarcastically claims similar relationship with famous dolphin
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation of animal friendship claims with specific vacation reference that makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Security Guard
dialogue.line: Oh, you're friends with a monkey? Well, I'm best friends with Flipper.

jokes.text: Whoa, dude. Burn!
jokes.setup: Monkey trainer calls Ross 'Harry Belafonte' mockingly
jokes.punchline: Crew member appreciates the insult
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate slang reaction to insult - functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Monkey Trainer
dialogue.line: Hey Harry Belafonte!

dialogue.speaker: Crew Member
dialogue.line: Hey, that's not an insult!

jokes.text: In human terms? I'd say... Cybill Shepherd.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks how big a star Marcel is
jokes.punchline: Compares monkey's fame level to moderately successful actress
jokes.explanation: Solid comparison joke that puts monkey stardom in perspective with specific, mid-tier celebrity reference.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How big a star is Marcel?

dialogue.speaker: Trainer
dialogue.line: Well, he's been on TV a few times. He's about as famous as a moderately successful actress.

jokes.text: It looks like her eyebrow fell down.
jokes.setup: Discussing actress's mustache problem
jokes.punchline: Describes facial hair as misplaced eyebrow
jokes.explanation: Vivid visual description that's both specific and absurd - eyebrow falling down is perfect imagery.

dialogue.speaker: Makeup Artist
dialogue.line: You have a mustache.

dialogue.speaker: Makeup Artist
dialogue.line: It's like a misplaced eyebrow.

jokes.text: I graduated fourth grade and realized I wasn't a pimp.
jokes.setup: Susie mentions Chandler's old hat with mirrors
jokes.punchline: Chandler explains his childhood fashion evolution
jokes.explanation: Great character backstory joke that explains childhood fashion choice through adult perspective.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, the mirror hat! Yeah, well, I was going through a phase. I thought if I wore enough mirrors, people would be so distracted by their own reflections, they wouldn't notice how awkward I was.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It didn't work. Turns out people just saw themselves looking confused.

jokes.text: Rachel, he totally changed time!
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if Van Damme was good in Time Cop
jokes.punchline: Monica treats time travel action movie as documentary
jokes.explanation: Monica's literal interpretation of action movie plots as if they were real achievements.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Was Van Damme good in Time Cop?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, he was great! I learned so much about time travel.

jokes.text: You can tell her I think her friend is cute.
jokes.setup: Monica tells Van Damme that Rachel thinks he's cute
jokes.punchline: Van Damme deflects back to Monica instead of Rachel
jokes.explanation: Standard wingman situation reversal where the messenger becomes the target.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel thinks you're cute.

dialogue.speaker: Van Damme
dialogue.line: Rachel? What about you?

jokes.text: And if things go well, maybe this time... I'll get to see your underwear.
jokes.setup: Susie asking Chandler on date
jokes.punchline: References the childhood incident that embarrassed her
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that promises revenge while setting up romantic possibility.

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: So Chandler, do you remember that time in high school when you pantsed me in front of everyone?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh God, Susie, I'm so sorry about that. I was just trying to be funny.

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: Well, you know what? I'm over it. And I'd like to go out with you.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really? That's great!

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: Yeah... so meet me at the coffee shop tomorrow at 8. And Chandler? Don't be late, or I'll pants you.

jokes.text: If I ask you to, you'll end up drinking it yourself.
jokes.setup: Rachel offers to get Monica's espresso
jokes.punchline: Monica doesn't trust Rachel's self-control around coffee
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about Rachel's known weakness for consuming things meant for others.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'll get your espresso.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, that's okay.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Because you'll drink it.

jokes.text: Okay, then eat me, I'm done!
jokes.setup: Trying to explain vegetable doneness testing to Monica
jokes.punchline: Adapts meat cooking phrase to vegetarian context
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's creative adaptation of cooking metaphors to fit her vegetarian worldview.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You just stick a fork in it to see if it's done.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A fork?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, like you do with meat. 'If it's pierced easily, it's done.' Except with vegetables it's more like 'If it's pierced easily, it's... really dead.'

jokes.text: Well, yes, actually... but they were my Aunt Edna's. And there were three of us in there.
jokes.setup: Susie asks if Chandler's ever worn women's underwear
jokes.punchline: Chandler admits to it but reveals bizarre family circumstance
jokes.explanation: Escalates from simple cross-dressing admission to weird family sharing situation that's somehow worse.

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: Have you ever worn women's underwear?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No! Well, once, but it was a very special circumstance.

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: What circumstance?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My parents were very weird.

jokes.text: We passed moment we should stop holding hands.
jokes.setup: Monica and Rachel walking while holding hands
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges the social awkwardness but continues anyway
jokes.explanation: Self-aware cringe humor about adult women holding hands past appropriate duration.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: People are staring.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We should probably let go.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, probably.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: On the count of three.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: One... two... three.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ready?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Not really.

jokes.text: If we were in prison, you guys would be, like, my bitches.
jokes.setup: Monica successfully breaks up Rachel and Phoebe's fight
jokes.punchline: Monica claims dominance using prison hierarchy metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica power trip moment - takes control then fantasizes about prison dominance hierarchy.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's right. I'm the queen bee now. You two are going to have to fight for my attention in the yard.

jokes.text: Because I went to an all-boys high school, and God is making up for it?
jokes.setup: Susie asks why Chandler can only think about licking ice off her
jokes.punchline: Chandler attributes sexual opportunity to divine compensation
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler logic linking his sexual frustration to educational background and karmic justice.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because God is good!

jokes.text: I hope you realize this means we'll miss hearing about the specials.
jokes.setup: Susie wanting Chandler to strip in restaurant bathroom
jokes.punchline: Chandler focuses on missing mundane restaurant experience
jokes.explanation: Chandler's priorities during sexual encounter - worried about missing restaurant specials announcement.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. You want me to take my clothes off in a public restaurant bathroom? Do you have any idea what that's like? I mean, besides the obvious humiliation and the fact that I'd probably get arrested, do you know how cold those bathroom tiles are? And the lighting! The fluorescent lighting in a restaurant bathroom is designed to make you look like you're already dead. I'm not stripping in there. I'm going to go back out there, order a nice salad, and pretend this conversation never happened.

jokes.text: This is for the fourth grade.
jokes.setup: Susie has successfully gotten Chandler in underwear in public
jokes.punchline: Reveals this was elaborate revenge for childhood humiliation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant long-term revenge plot reveal. Perfect structure from setup to payoff across the episode.

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: You thought you were so funny in third grade, didn't you? Well, how do you like it now, Bing?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Susie, come on! That was like twenty years ago!

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: I've had a long memory and a lot of free time.

jokes.text: Call me in 20 years and tell me if you're still upset about this.
jokes.setup: Chandler questioning why Susie is still upset about fourth grade
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests time will heal wounds while creating new trauma
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Chandler dismisses holding grudges while creating a new reason for Susie to hold one.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Come on, Susie, that was fourth grade. Time heals all wounds.

dialogue.speaker: Susie
dialogue.line: Not this one.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, time will create new wounds that distract you from this one.

jokes.text: Rachel told me... you were dying to have a threesome with me and Drew Barrymore.
jokes.setup: Van Damme explaining why he agreed to blind date
jokes.punchline: Reveals Monica's wingwoman attempt created unrealistic expectations
jokes.explanation: Escalation of celebrity dating expectations to absurd threesome with another celebrity.

dialogue.speaker: Van Damme
dialogue.line: Your friend told me you were smart, beautiful, and had a great sense of humor.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I am all of those things!

dialogue.speaker: Van Damme
dialogue.line: I know. That's the problem. She set the bar so high, I thought you'd be even better.

jokes.text: You always wear panties? No, no. This is the first time.
jokes.setup: Joey discovering Chandler in women's underwear
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defensive clarification makes it sound worse
jokes.explanation: Chandler's attempt to clarify only makes the situation more embarrassing and suspicious.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, what are you doing in women's underwear?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not in women's underwear!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Then what is that?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: These are my underwear. I'm a woman.

jokes.text: I'm getting heat from the guy in the hot pink thong.
jokes.setup: Joey refusing to give Chandler his underwear
jokes.punchline: Points out Chandler's lack of authority given his current situation
jokes.explanation: Joey correctly points out the irony of Chandler making demands while wearing pink women's underwear.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You can't tell me what to do! You're not the boss of me!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, I'm asking nicely—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, that's rich coming from a guy who can't even get his own underwear!

jokes.text: Keep your panties on.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking for milk Monica is finishing
jokes.punchline: Monica uses common expression that's literally applicable to Chandler
jokes.explanation: Perfect accidental callback - common expression becomes literally applicable to Chandler's situation.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, can I have some of that milk?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, sorry, this is the last of it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh come on, there's plenty left.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know, but Chandler's been drinking a lot of milk lately. He's going through it like there's no tomorrow.

jokes.text: Chandler, can I borrow your G-string? How long you been waiting to say that? About 20 minutes!
jokes.setup: Phoebe needs guitar string, uses double entendre
jokes.punchline: Chandler appreciates her patience in setting up the joke
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about joke construction - Phoebe waited for right moment to deploy her musical double entendre.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I need a new G string.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I appreciate you taking the time to set that up.

jokes.text: Mommy! Can't you see what's going on? This man is dead!
jokes.setup: Joey repeatedly failing his line about a dying man
jokes.punchline: Finally gets frustrated and changes to simpler, wrong line
jokes.explanation: Joey's acting limitations shown through his inability to deliver simple line, then giving up and changing it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, the horror!

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.line: Cut! Joey, the line is 'The anguish of my deeds will haunt me forever.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The anguish of my deeds will haunt me... forever. The anguish of my deeds will haunt me forever. The anguish... ugh!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? Forget it. How about this: 'Joey likes to eat'?

tmdb_overview: Ross goes to visit Marcel, his monkey, while on a trip to California. Joey gets his first fan letter and ends up dating the woman. Phoebe is asked to sing for the children at the library, but their parents are horrified by her lyrics.
insight: Super Bowl episode lands 58 jokes in 45 minutes but underperforms impact expectations at 76.5.
